summary: Completed read-only audit of MCP runtime freshness/version parity. Package, package-lock, src/dist OS contract, and live MCP responses all report 2.2.1, and installed Codex config points to the local dist/index.js with portable Node v24.18.0 and data root <machine-local-path> Concrete blockers found: many long-running DinoBrain node MCP processes remain alive from before the current dist/index.js build timestamp, making runtime freshness nondeterministic; live task/event observability has mixed 2.2.0 and 2.2.1 records today; latest codex_preflight_completed report was a diagnostic probe rather than proof of a real user-prompt hook; and the worktree is dirty with package.json/install.ps1 modifications that should be accounted for before claiming a clean release/runtime parity state.
decisions: Kept audit read-only and did not edit repo files.
Treated diagnostic hook reports as weaker evidence than a real prompt-time preflight.
Classified stale pre-build MCP node processes as the main runtime freshness blocker.
next_steps: Stop or reload stale DinoBrain MCP node processes and restart/new Codex session so all live MCP processes start after the latest dist build.
Run a fresh real-prompt live preflight verification and require codex_prompt_submitted/codex_preflight_completed evidence before manual MCP calls.
Resolve or intentionally keep the dirty package.json/install.ps1 changes before release/runtime parity signoff.
